title.en: Are you kidding me? 지금 장난해? 농담이지?

description.en: When you can't believe what someone is saying! Learn how to express disbelief using "Jang-nan-hae?" and "Nong-dam-i-ji?".
description.ko: 상대방의 말이 도저히 믿기지 않을 때! "장난해?"와 "농담이지?"를 사용해 황당함을 표현하는 방법을 배웁니다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world. Welcome back to Talkori. I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕하세요 여러분~! 오늘도 유쾌한 에너지로 돌아온 미나입니다.
content.translation: Hello everyone~! I'm the cheerful Mina back again today.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. I decided to sell my house. and move into a tent. In the middle of Gangnam.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네~? 강남 한복판에서 텐트요?
content.translation: What~? A tent in the middle of Gangnam?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 지금 장난해요~?
content.translation: John..., 'Are you kidding me right now~?'

insight.title: 장난해?
insight.usage_tip: Used when someone is acting or speaking in an absurd or unbelievable way.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "Are you kidding me?" No! I already bought the tent. It has a chimney!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아 진짜. 농담이죠? 제발 농담이라고 말해줘요!
content.translation: Ah..., seriously 'You're joking. right?' Please tell me you're joking!

insight.title: 농담이지?
insight.usage_tip: Used when you hope the other person's shocking statement is just a joke.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "장난" and "농담". They both mean joking. but is there a difference.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 음..., "장난"은 행동이나 프랭크에 더 가까워요.
content.translation: 'Jang-nan' is closer to actions or pranks.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그래서 "장난해?" 라고 하면 'Are you playing with me?' 라는 느낌이죠.
content.translation: So. if you say 'Jang-nan-hae?'. it's like 'Are you playing with me?'

content.speaker: John
content.text: "장난해~?"
content.translation: Are you kidding me~?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그리고 "농담"은 말로 하는 농담이에요.
content.translation: And 'Nong-dam' is a joke made with words.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "농담이지?" 라고 물어보면 상대방의 말이 사실이 아니길 바라는 마음이 담겨있어요.
content.translation: If you ask 'Nong-dam-i-ji?'. it contains a wish that the person's words aren't true.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "농담이지...,"
content.translation: You're joking. right...,

content.speaker: John
content.text: Imagine I told you that I just won the lottery. but I lost the ticket.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 헉! "진짜요~?" 얼마짜리였는데요?
content.translation: Huh! 'Really~?' How much was it for?

content.speaker: John
content.text: One million dollars. I think I used it as a bookmark. and then I donated the book.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "아..., 진짜!" 존 씨. "지금 장난해요~?"
content.translation: Ah..., seriously! John. 'Are you kidding me right now~?'

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'm not kidding. I'm looking for the book right now.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "설마..., 농담이죠?" 제발 그렇다고 하세요!
content.translation: Surely..., 'you're joking. right?' Please say so!

content.speaker: John
content.text: "Are you kidding me?" Of course it's a joke! I didn't even buy a ticket.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아~ 진짜. 사람 놀리지 마요! 정말 기가 막히네!
content.translation: Ah~ 'Stop teasing people!' Truly breathtaking!

insight.title: 사람 놀리다
insight.description: To tease someone / To make fun of someone
insight.usage_tip: Used when someone is pranked or fooled.

content.speaker: John
content.text: These reactions are so essential when your friends are being silly.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 상대방이 엉뚱한 소리를 할 때 오늘 배운 표현들을 꼭 써보세요!
content.translation: Right. Make sure to use the expressions we learned today when someone says something out of left field!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "kidding" reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number one. "장난해?"
content.translation: Are you kidding me?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number two. "농담이지?"
content.translation: You're joking. right?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number three. "지금 장난해요?"
content.translation: Are you kidding me right now?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number four. "농담하지 마요~!"
content.translation: Don't joke around~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number five. "진담이야?"
content.translation: Are you serious? / Is it for real?

insight.title: 진담이야?
insight.description: Asking if it's the 'Truth' as opposed to a 'Joke'
insight.usage_tip: Used to check if someone is being sincere.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분 주변에는 기분 좋은 농담만 가득하길 바랄게요!
content.translation: I hope your surroundings are filled with only pleasant jokes!

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 장난이 너무 심해요. (The prank is too much.)
방금 한 말은 농담이었어요. (What I just said was a joke.)
설마 그게 진짜일까요? (Surely that can't be true?)
저는 지금 진담이에요. (I'm serious right now.)
저를 그만 놀리세요. (Please stop teasing me.)

vocabulary.meaning: In the middle of. Heart of
vocabulary.example: 도시 한복판에서 길을 잃었어요. (I got lost in the middle of the city.)
